metadata.text: Rector of President University: The current Rector of President University (2024-2027) is Handa S. Abidin, S.H., LL.M., Ph.D. He is an expert in International Law and previously served as Vice Rector for Academics. He was officially inaugurated as Rector on January 31, 2024, succeeding Prof. Dr. Chairy.

President University - Home Faculty of Business Faculty of Artificial Intelligence and Smart Manufacturing Faculty of Engineering Faculty of Social Science and Education Faculty of Law Faculty of Medicine Faculty of Art, Design, and Architecture Where Our Graduates Work. Our graduates thrive in business and arts, often continuing their studies or launching ventures. We highlight companies hiring them and our robust alumni network. Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc., Ratih Megasari Singkarru, M.Sc., is an Indonesian politician and House of Representatives member (2019-2024). A 2008 Communication Science graduate from President University, she is also President Director of PT Ratih Graha Persada and PT Ratih Graha Cipta, and Commissioner of PT Dirga Mega Cipta. Felicia Putri Tjiasaka, a management alumna, is a content creator and Co-Founder of Ternak Uang, known for her investment content on social media. She was a runner-up at the 2015 DSB Asian Conference-Young Economist Stand Up. Irene Umar is an outstanding alumna of the Management Program at President University. With extensive experience in finance, investment, and social sectors through her roles at Standard Chartered Bank and W3GG, she currently serves as Vice Minister/Deputy Head of Creative Economy of the Republic of Indonesia. Graha Yudha Andarano Putra P After graduating, Graha Yudha worked in finance, including at Citi Bank. By age 33, he joined the boards of state-owned enterprises and became a member of Indonesia’s Presidential Advisory Council, a role he still holds today. He holds a B.Sc. from President University, an MBA from Strathclyde, and is a PhD candidate at the University of Glasgow’s Adam Smith Business School. After graduating, Darren immediately entered the field of Search Engine Optimization. Working closely with his team, he played a pivotal role in leading PT Sumber Sinergi Makmur through its Initial Public Offering (IPO) in October 2023, where he serves as the Chairman of the board. He holds a B.Sc. in Industrial Engineering from President University.

Student Affairs:
President University - Student Affairs Student Affairs Bureau is the core that energizes campus life, dedicated to nurturing every student into a well-rounded leader. Student Affairs does not simply manage services, but also actively cultivates an ecosystem full of opportunities and support. Our mission goes beyond managing activities. It's about embedding the core values of President University, especially the philosophy, into every aspect of campus life. We are the architects of a dynamic, multicultural, and high-impact student experience. Our key areas of focus include: Fostering and guiding student organizations (PUSC, PUSB, PUFA, PUMA), clubs and communities (CnC). Overseeing major events and leadership training. Develops leadership, teamwork, and organizational skills as we provides avenues for expression and contribution to campus life. Through President University Student Council (PUSC) and President University Student Board (PUSB), we host vibrant and high-impact events designed to challenge student to develop essential skills, and celebrate the university’s unique Culture. These student-driven initiatives, which form a vital part of the Portfolio-Building Curriculum, include the intensive President Youth Leadership Camp ( , the artistic showcase of President University Vocal Victory , the industry insights platform , the competitive spirit of President University Sport Tournament , the global celebration of Cultural Festival CULFEST), and the culminating recognition at the Student Awarding Night These events are crucial for fostering collaboration among students and implementing the Medium of Communication in English in a dynamic, fun setting. We encourage student involvement in competitions or the hosting of national and international level events, providing unmatched real-world experience and networking opportunities.We actively facilitate student applications for prestigious scholarships provided by our industry and government partners, including: a. Adaro Foundation Scholarship: A program supporting students in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields, focusing on academic excellence and leadership potential. b. China Harbour Engineering Company (CHEC) Financial aid specifically aimed at students demonstrating high academic merit, often prioritizing those in relevant engineering or technical disciplines. c. Beasiswa Pemerintah Daerah (Banpin Pemda) Bekasi Scholarships provided by the Bekasi Local Government to support outstanding students who are residents of Bekasi, promoting local talent and development. Diktisaintek Berdampak is a continuous program for univeristy students across Indonesia. The program include: Providing essential services such as development programs, counseling referrals, and maintaining student records. Ethical Conduct & Policy Developing and implementing non-academic policies, including student conduct procedures, to ensure a respectful and orderly campus environment. Student life at President University is vibrant and community-focused. PUSH, the university dormitory, offers a supportive living environment for students. Meanwhile, PUSU (student union) and CnC (Clubs and Communities) provide opportunities for leadership, creativity, and meaningful engagement on campus.

Endowment Fund:
President University - Endowment Fund Securing Education’s Future by Investing in Endowment Today. President University Endowment Fund is a program to raise funds through contributions from student, alumni, the academic community, companies, philanthropists and other sources. Then the funds are invested and the results are used to support the sustainability of President University in the future. Additional Contribution in 2026 As of December 31, 2025 Making an endowment donation for President University is easy. Use the QR Code attached below or you can also transfer it manually through our Bank Account and please save your transaction proof and send it to our email at alumni@president.ac.id (include your name and phone number). Yayasan Pend Universitas Presiden Yay Pendidikan Univ Presiden Since its founding, President University has produced more than 11.000 alumni spread across the world. Alumni are a very valuable asset for President University. The active participation of alumni in the progress of their alma mater is highly expected. Company is a strategic partner for President University. Industry will later absorb the human resources produced by President University. Therefore, President University really hopes that Industry will participate in investing in human resource development in the future through President University donations. Organizations or communities can also contribute to the progress of President University. Various organizations and communities have shown their commitment to raising funds for President University. Both lecturers and staff are also expected to be able to contribute, either donating funds or initiating fundraising movements in order to increase the President University Endowment Fund. President University really hopes for joint contributions from the wider community who care about higher education. Any individual can contribute by donating some of their funds for the development of science and technology. This endowment fund is designed to provide support for the continuity of President University's operations, not only for the current generation but also for the future. With the determination to maintain the continuity of existence across generations, the management of these funds will be carried out carefully, ensuring that the benefits can be enjoyed by future generations. Some of the objectives of this endowment fund are as attached below: Part of the funds from the Endowment Fund will be used to provide scholarships to students with achievements and potential, ensuring access to quality education without financial barriers. Academic and Research Development Funds will be allocated to support research activities, seminars and workshops to improve academic excellence and produce innovations that are beneficial to society. Facilities and Infrastructure Development A portion of the funds will be invested in developing campus facilities and infrastructure, creating a modern and adequate learning environment.

OER – Adam Kurniawan Library:
OER – Adam Kurniawan Library Connect, Covered, Get Inspired | NPP: 3216092D0000001 OPEN EDUCATIONAL RESOURCES (OER) Learning and teaching materials that can be shared, edited, and reused without obtaining permission from the owner are known as Open Educational Resources, or OERs. Since the producer of OER material has lifted certain copyright restrictions, it is readily accessible to the public and uses under an open license in the public domain. For academics looking for a free and authorized alternative information source to aid in their research and study, President University OER is available. The following are the tenets of OER: OER documents can be downloaded, printed, and saved using retain. Reuse refers to the acceptance of content reuse. Using revise, you can modify the material to fit your requirements. Blending several OER materials together to create a new work is made feasible by remix. Redistribute refers to the sharing of content in either its original or altered format. Uraian Tugas - SPMI Universitas:
Uraian Tugas - SPMI Universitas Direktorat Penjaminan Mutu Internal Merumuskan kebijakan strategis terkait penjaminan mutu internal yang mencakup standar, prosedur, dan mekanisme evaluasi. Mengimplementasikan kebijakan ini secara efektif di seluruh unit kerja universitas. Menyusun dan memimpin pelaksanaan evaluasi dan audit mutu internal secara berkala, termasuk meninjau program studi, fakultas, dan unit administrasi dalam hal pencapaian standar mutu. Bekerjasama dengan unit terkait untuk menyusun rencana perbaikan berdasarkan hasil evaluasi dan audit. Memastikan bahwa langkah-langkah perbaikan dilaksanakan sesuai dengan rekomendasi yang diberikan. Menyusun laporan berkala terkait hasil penjaminan mutu dan menyampaikan rekomendasi untuk perbaikan atau pengambilan keputusan kepada Rektor dan pimpinan universitas lainnya. Mengkoordinasikan semua unit di universitas agar siap menghadapi proses akreditasi, baik nasional maupun internasional. Mengelola dokumentasi dan proses persiapan akreditasi secara komprehensif. Menginisiasi dan memfasilitasi program pelatihan dan pengembangan kapasitas sumber daya manusia yang terlibat dalam penjaminan mutu, termasuk dosen, tenaga kependidikan, dan staf administrasi. Menjalin hubungan dengan lembaga penjaminan mutu eksternal, seperti BAN-PT dan lembaga akreditasi internasional, untuk memastikan bahwa standar dan praktik di universitas selaras dengan regulasi dan Mengawasi pelaksanaan sistem penjaminan mutu di setiap program studi dan unit kerja. Melakukan monitoring berkelanjutan terhadap pelaksanaan standar dan prosedur yang telah ditetapkan. Mempromosikan budaya peningkatan mutu yang berkelanjutan di lingkungan universitas melalui penguatan komitmen semua stakeholder terhadap pencapaian kualitas terbaik di semua aspek kegiatan. WAKIL DIREKTUR BIDANG AKREDITASI Memimpin persiapan universitas dalam menjalani proses akreditasi nasional untuk program studi, fakultas, dan universitas secara keseluruhan. Berita - SPMI Universitas:
Berita - SPMI Universitas Direktorat Penjaminan Mutu Internal Berita & Kegiatan SPMI Universitas Presiden Mengadakan Penguatan Sistem Penjaminan Mutu Internal Menuju Prof. Zainal Arifin Hasibuan selaku narasumber dan Ibu Ir. Mia Galina sebagai Moderator Cikarang, 6 Februari 2026 – Universitas Presiden menyelenggarakan kegiatan Penguatan Sistem Penjaminan Mutu Internal (SPMI) bertempat di Auditorium Charles Himawan Lantai 5. Kegiatan ini dihadiri oleh pejabat struktural, dosen, dan tenaga kependidikan dari lingkungan universitas, serta menghadirkan narasumber kompeten dari Universitas IPB, Prof. Zainal Arifin Hasibuan, Wakil Rektor IV Kegiatan ini dilaksanakan sebagai bagian dari upaya institusi untuk memperkuat pemahaman seluruh sivitas akademika mengenai implementasi Sistem Penjaminan Mutu Internal sesuai dengan ketentuan terbaru dalam Permendikti Saintek Nomor 39 Tahun 2025. Melalui kegiatan ini, diharapkan Universitas Presiden dapat terus mempertahankan standar mutu yang telah diraih dan menyesuaikan diri terhadap dinamika regulasi perguruan Dalam sambutannya, Ketua Direktorat Penjaminan Mutu Internal Universitas Presiden menegaskan bahwa peningkatan mutu pendidikan tinggi harus berfokus pada substansi mutu, bukan sekadar aspek administratif. Universitas Presiden berkomitmen untuk mengembangkan budaya mutu yang adaptif, berkelanjutan, dan berbasis evaluasi mandiri yang objektif. Pemaparan materi oleh Prof. Zainal Arifin Hasibuan Berbagai pendekatan strategis seperti penerapan Outcome-Based Education (OBE), penguatan Project-Based Learning, serta peningkatan profesionalisme dosen menjadi prioritas dalam mencapai standar mutu yang unggul dan berorientasi pada hasil. Selain itu, evaluasi diri berbasis baseline dan penetapan target peningkatan menjadi elemen penting dalam menjaga dan meningkatkan capaian mutu secara sistematis. Kegiatan ini menegaskan bahwa peran SPMI sebagai pengendali dan penggerak siklus PPEPP (Perencanaan, Pelaksanaan, Evaluasi, Pengendalian, dan Peningkatan) sangat krusial dalam memastikan seluruh standar mutu dapat diterapkan secara efektif dan berkelanjutan. Universitas Presiden berharap, melalui penguatan sistem ini, institusi dapat mempertahankan keunggulan dan daya saing secara nasional maupun internasional, serta mampu menghasilkan lulusan yang berkualitas tinggi dan relevan dengan kebutuhan dunia kerja. Foto bersama jajaran pimpinan dan peserta kegiatan Universitas Katolik Widya Mandala Surabaya Sukses Laksanakan Benchmarking SPMI dengan President University Suasana kegiatan benchmarking antara UKWMS dan President University Surabaya, 22 Agustus 2025 – Universitas Katolik Widya Mandala Surabaya (UKWMS) sukses melaksanakan benchmarking Sistem Penjaminan Mutu Internal (SPMI) dengan President University pada Jumat, 22 Agustus 2025. Kegiatan ini bertujuan memperkuat sistem mutu perguruan tinggi melalui pertukaran pengalaman mengenai strategi budaya mutu, mekanisme audit internal, serta inovasi pengelolaan mutu yang berkelanjutan. Hasilnya, UKWMS menunjukkan keunggulan dalam pembangunan budaya mutu yang tanggung jawab di setiap prodi dan fakultas, serta keberadaan Gugus Jaminan Mutu (GJM) yang berfungsi sebagai penghubung kebijakan mutu universitas dengan implementasi di tingkat fakultas dan program studi. Pemaparan dokumentasi mutu dan sistem organisasi oleh UKWMS Dalam pelaksanaan benchmarking tersebut, UKWMS memaparkan dokumentasi mutu yang komprehensif, mulai dari dokumen siklus penetapan, laporan evaluasi, hingga dokumen tambahan seperti OTK dan AMI. Sistem organisasi dan kebijakan mutu yang terstruktur juga menjadi salah satu keunggulan, di mana seluruh proses didukung oleh struktur organisasi yang jelas dan kolaboratif. Kegiatan ini tidak hanya memperkuat reputasi mutu UKWMS, tetapi juga membuka peluang kolaborasi jangka panjang dengan President University dalam pengembangan sistem mutu dan peningkatan daya saing. Foto bersama dalam kegiatan benchmarking SPMI Kegiatan benchmarking ini diharapkan mampu menjadi langkah strategis dalam memperkuat budaya mutu dan tata kelola pendidikan tinggi yang berkelanjutan. Melalui pertukaran pengalaman dan praktik terbaik, kedua universitas berharap dapat menciptakan inovasi dalam pengelolaan mutu dan memperluas jejaring kolaboratif. Hasil dari kegiatan ini diharapkan dapat dijadikan dasar untuk rencana aksi nyata yang mendukung peningkatan kualitas internal dan memperkuat posisi institusi dalam persaingan global serta memenuhi standar akreditasi nasional dan internasional. History:
President University - History The conceptual plan of President University was first formulated in September 1997 by S. D. Darmono, the Chairman of Jababeka Group and Prof. Donald W. Watts, who was the President of Bond University, Queensland and Vice Chancellor of Curtin University, Western Australia. The goal of their proposed University was to create an institution of learning which would prepare future leaders of industry and society by giving them the skills and experience necessary to excel upon graduation. The institution officially operated in 2001, offering a Bachelor of Engineering degree. At that time, the institution was called the Cikarang School of Engineering (Sekolah Tinggi Teknik Cikarang). On April 16, 2004, the Ministry of Education granted President University official status as a full-fledged university. Although as a relatively young university, President University (PresUniv) is growing at a tremendous rate every year. There are now approximately 10,000 students attending President University from Indonesia as well as many other countries. President University has expanded its academic disciplines and now offers 26 majors in 7 different faculties, ranging from Art, Design & Architecture, Business, Artificial Intelligence and Smart Manufacturing, Engineering, Law, Medicine, and Social Science & Education. "To be an eminent university that produces leaders with entrepreneurial spirit, multicultural and global mindset." About – Adam Kurniawan Library:
About – Adam Kurniawan Library Connect, Covered, Get Inspired | NPP: 3216092D0000001 Connect, Covered, Get Inspired Initiated by Mr. S.D. Darmono, one of the founders of President University, the university library was first opened in 2003. As the university grew, a bigger space was needed to house the large collection of books and materials as well serve the information needs of the library users and as a result, it doubled in size. The library was officially named The Adam Kurniawan Library in honor of Adam Kurniawan, who was warmly known as “Pak Adam” by his friends and business associates. He was a respected entrepreneur in the Indonesian textile industry and a co-founder of the Jababeka Group. He knew the importance of helping others to achieve success in their lives. He was an important figure who had a great concern for the nation’s education, the university and its library. To help manage the library system as well as all the collection of books and materials, an IT-based library information system called Suteki was installed and implemented in 2007 to serve the campus community as well as all the library users. Under the coordination and fund-raising activities of Dr. Agus Canny, the Vice Rector III of President University, donations of library materials have been given from many sources such as private institutions, Jababeka business partners, PATA, Indonesian ministries, Asia Foundation, LPPM, Bank Indonesia and other organizations. Leaders of President University from the rectors, heads of departments, faculty staff even the alumni of the university have also been involved in President University has grown a lot in the past few years and it recently became important to expand the space of the library, the books and materials as well as its services to accommodate the growing student body and achieve the vision of the university. The library started renovations in May 2016 and it is now ready to be used as of August 2016 with its new phase of “home” and “services” to serve the academic and literature needs of the university. The design concept of the new library celebrates the UNESCO 4 pillars of Education: ‘Learning to Know’ ‘Learning to Do’ ‘Learning to Be’ and ‘Learning to Live together’. These four pillars of education are represented in the library by the four pillars of bookshelves and 4 big crystal lamps hanging on the four corners above the book collection, as the heart of the library. The pillars in the library are made of concrete and each pillar is covered with two mirrors with bare concrete in the middle, and is equipped with wall lamps. The bare concrete symbolizes that knowledge and wisdom starts from “raw” data while the wall lamps represent education. These pillars with bare concrete, mirrors and lamps indicate that “raw” data can be found in the library materials and it will be transformed into information and knowledge through education which can be shared and multiplied by others. It is hoped that the library will be used to maximize the learning process of our students as a fundamental part of their academic and research endeavors at President University. International Relations:
President University - International Relations President University’s International Relations program produces "The Global Strategist"—a professional who combines the vision of a statesman, the precision of an analyst, with the speed of an entrepreneur. We don’t just teach IR — we shape leaders. We offer an optimized 3-Year pathway to complete 144 SKS, allowing high-achievers to enter the workforce or postgraduate studies 1.5 years ahead of traditional undergraduate and even International Undergraduate Program peers. Jababeka as the ‘Living Laboratory’: Direct immersion in Southeast Asia’s largest industrial hub with 2,000+ MNCs, where students engage in Year 1 business projects and corporate diplomacy in a truly international community. This is IR education embedded in industry, not isolated in classrooms. Elite Access & Global Immersion: President University IR transforms global affairs into a daily professional reality through weekly briefings with Foreign Ambassadors, UN Directors, and executives from top-tier MNCs, offering elite mentorship that transcends traditional textbooks. This immersive experience is powered by a 30-nation campus community and a third-year Global Mobility Program, ensuring students master intercultural diplomacy in a real-world "Mini-UN" environment before they even graduate. We replace passive learning with , including specialized training in Sustainability (ESG) Reporting, Country Risk Analysis, Policy Brief Writing, and Strategic Negotiation. A foundational 2-semester package in Mandarin, Japanese, French, or Spanish, with an optional Professional Certification Semester to secure globally recognized credentials (HSK, JLPT, DELF). Your final project is a professional result, not just a thesis. Whether launching a social venture or conducting a risk audit for an embassy or MNC, you graduate with a Portfolio of Outcomes that ensures you are "Recruited Before Graduation.” 3 - 4 years Theory, Foundations & Research Methods This cluster oversees students' academic rigor and methodological training.Foundation & Theory: Foundation in Social Sciences, Introduction to Political Science, Introduction to International Relations, and IR Theories.Methodology: Statistics and Data Literacy and IR Research Methods.Academic Writing: First-Year Writing Seminar and Academic Journal Writing and Publishing. Diplomacy & Global Governance This cluster focuses on the practice of statecraft and the role of international institutions. Core Practice: Global Leadership, Diplomacy and Ambassadorial Practice, and IR Seminar.Institutions & Law: Principles of International Law and International Organizations, Global Governance and the UN.Policy & Analysis: Foreign Policy Analysis and Indonesian Foreign and Security Policy.Professional Skills: Policy Brief Writing, Negotiation Technique and Lobbying, and Diplomatic Protocol. Security, Strategy & Conflict Studies This cluster explores how states and non-state actors use various strategies— from military power to diplomacy and intelligence — to manage, resolve, or engage in conflicts. Core Security: International Security. Thematic Electives: Study of War and Peace, Defense Policy, Analysis of Strategic Domain and Seapower and Maritime Affairs. Modern Threats: Security Revolution and Cyber Security and Conflict Studies. International Political Economy (IPE) & Corporate Diplomacy This Cluster bridges the gap between international politics, business, and economics.Core Economics: International Political Economy and Economic Survival (Gen-AI/Business Project).Corporate Track: Corporate Diplomacy, Business in World Politics, and the Corporate Diplomacy Bootcamp.Trade & Development: the Politics of International Trade and International Development Cooperation in Practice. Area & Regional Studies This cluster focuses on specific geographic and political landscapes.Regional Core: The Dynamics of ASEAN and Comparative Politics.Regional Electives: Politics of Europe and the EU, Domestic and Foreign Affairs of China, US Foreign Policy, and International Relations and Development in Africa.Specialized Areas:AreasDynamics of the MENA Region, East Asia in the World, and Discover Palestine. Global Issues & Sustainability (Transnational Studies) This cluster handles cross-cutting themes that affect the global community beyond state borders.Human Rights & Society: Human Rights and Human Security, Gender Studies, and International Migration.Environment: Environmental Issues in IR and Sustainable Development/Global Environmental Politics.Culture & Soft Power: International Culture and Communication, Popular Culture and Globalization, and Gastrodiplomacy. Policy Brief Writing, Negotiation Technique and Lobbying, and Diplomatic Protocol and Etiquette. Project Proposal and Concept Note Writing, and Sustainability Reporting. Country Risk, and Government Relations Skills. This lab is the primary training ground for the practical "hard skills" of international relations. It benefits IR students by providing a simulated environment to master the mechanics of statecraft—including public speaking, diplomatic protocol, crisis negotiation, and table-top exercises. This is where students physically practice the art of diplomacy through mock summits and role-play scenarios, ensuring they graduate with the confidence and technical competence required for real-world diplomatic missions and foreign ministry assignments.Crisis Negotiation - Ambassadorial Practice - Protocol Mastery - Mock Summits. President Center for International Studies (PRECIS) PRECIS operates as the primary research think tank for the School of International Relations, specifically designed to cultivate students' academic and analytical rigor in strategic studies, global security, and foreign policy. For IR students, this facility is invaluable as a launchpad for scholarly publication and policy research, offering opportunities to work directly alongside senior experts on high-level projects ranging from maritime defense doctrines to counter-terrorism strategies. By engaging with PRECIS, students transition from passive learners to active researchers, gaining privileged access to assistantships, mentorship for publishing in reputable journals like AEGIS, and the critical analytical skills required for careers in intelligence, government analysis, and academia.Research hub for strategic studies - security analysis - foreign policy research. President Center for UN Studies This facility functions as a specialized academic hub dedicated to the research and promotion of the United Nations' core values, specifically the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Unlike a simulation lab, this center benefits IR students by offering a rigorous academic environment for policy analysis and deep-dive research into global governance challenges. It provides students with opportunities to engage in high-level discourse on human rights, peace-building, and international development, preparing them for careers as policy advisors, UN staff members, or researchers who need a profound theoretical understanding of the UN system rather than just procedural mechanics.SDGs - Human Rights - Peace-building. Indonesia-China Partnership Institution (ICPI-PU) The ICPI-PU functions as a specialized strategic hub that allows IR students to examine the geopolitical and economic intricacies of one of the Asia-Pacific's most critical bilateral relationships. For aspiring diplomats and analysts, this institution offers a front-row seat to the dynamics of Sino-Indonesian cooperation, ranging from trade policy to cultural soft power. The facility benefits students by cultivating deep regional expertise and cross-cultural competence, ensuring they are competitive candidates for careers involving East Asian foreign policy, international trade, and transnational corporate relations.Sino-Indonesian relations - trade diplomacy - cultural soft power. Supports academic and research activities by providing access to books, academic journals, and digital resources relevant to international relations and related disciplines. Interior Design:
President University - Interior Design Why Interior Design at President University? Many people think interior design is just about choosing colors, furniture, or making spaces “pretty.” In reality, interior design is about how people experience space, how comfortable they feel, how they move, how they work, how they interact, and how a space supports their well-being, safety, and activities. Our program is designed around this deeper understanding of space and people. 1.Progressive, studio-based learning from homes to public spaces You don’t start with complicated buildings on day one. You begin with spaces people already understand: homes. In early studios, students learn how to design residential interiors such as bedrooms, living rooms, kitchens focusing on: how light, air, and materials affect comfort, and how layout influences daily life. As students progress, they move to more complex spaces, such as: hotels and mixed-use buildings, and This means learning grows step by step, just like real professional practice. By the end of the program, students are able to design spaces that serve many people, not just one family but also community areas, commercial spaces, or hospitality environments. 2. Strong industry alignment , more than decoration Interior designers today must understand much more than style. Our curriculum connects design with real industry needs, including: Sustainability — how to design spaces that save energy, reduce waste, and use responsible materials. Building systems — understanding lighting, ventilation, acoustics, and how buildings function so designs are safe and comfortable, not just beautiful. Professional practice — how real projects work: budgets, timelines, teamwork, communication with clients, and collaboration with architects and contractors. In simple terms: students learn how to design spaces that are beautiful, functional, safe, and responsible, these are the same expectations found in real design firms. President University - Facility Designed by Nick Faldo, Jababeka Golf & Country Club features an 18-hole course with full facilities, including a driving range, clubhouse, and event spaces for up to 200 guests. Jababeka Golf & Country Club, surrounded by lavish trees with magnificent shrubs and lush green fields, offers you the best backdrop for your business and recreation experiences. Designed by world-class golfer and architect Nick Faldo, Jababeka Golf and Country Club is just 30 minutes east of Jakarta with a lush green landscape in a region of exceptional serenity and exquisiteness. A masterful layout of 18 holes, Jababeka Golf Course and Country Club is truly a sight to behold, as it provides challenges and enjoyment to players of all skill levels — from beginner to professional — through the use of water features and special topography. The course holds the vision of its architect, where the design benefits from the same meticulous finishes found in Faldo’s playing method. It can be as challenging or as friendly as you like, through the wide choice of tees and the large variety of pin positions. A truly comprehensive facility, Jababeka Golf and Country Club features a golf pro-shop that carries the finest names in golf equipment and apparel, male and female locker rooms, and a restaurant that offers a wide array of excellent cuisine. There is also a full-service driving range, putting greens, a well-maintained swimming pool, two quality tennis courts, a children’s playground, massage service, and VIP and function rooms that can be tailored to your needs — whether for a sophisticated business meeting or a private party.

Facility: President University - Facility Our basketball court facilities are conveniently located across the campus and student dormitory areas, ensuring easy access for students to stay active and engaged. The facilities support student activities and competitive events, promoting fitness, teamwork, and an active campus lifestyle.

President University - Facility Adam Kurniawan Library at President University is a modern, accredited library supporting academic research with extensive print and digital collections, study spaces, and a BI Corner. The Adam Kurniawan Library at President University is a modern academic library named in honor of Adam Kurniawan, a respected entrepreneur and co-founder of the Jababeka Group. Established in 2003 and expanded in 2016, it serves as a central hub for learning and research, supporting the university's academic community. The library houses a wide range of materials, including textbooks, journals, fiction, student theses, and digital media. president.ac.id It offers comfortable study environments with general and individual study rooms, discussion areas, and free Wi-Fi to enhance the learning experience. An Online Public Access Catalog (OPAC) is available for users to search and access the library's resources remotely. In collaboration with Bank Indonesia, the library features a BI Corner, providing resources and information related to economics and finance. In March 2024, the library achieved an "A" rating from the National Library of the Republic of Indonesia, reflecting its excellence in services, collections, and facilities.

Facility: President University - Facility President Executive Club is a premium business club and hotel in Jababeka for networking and hospitality. President Executive Club (PEC) was founded by SD Darmono in June 2004 as a club for the meeting of minds and networking for President and Executives of 700 corporates in Cikarang Industrial Estate - Jababeka. The groundbreaking for the PEC building in Cikarang CBD was by Susilo Bambang Yudhoyono (Indonesian presidential candidate for 2004 election), Edward Lee (the Ambassador of Singapore) and Setyono Djuandi Darmono, attended by distinguished businessmen and top executives. In 2009, PEC was transformed to a national club with 11 founders, inaugurated by the Vice President at Ritz Carlton Jakarta.

Facility:
President University - Facility Charles Himawan Auditorium is a versatile event space at President University for seminars and student activities. The Charles Himawan Auditorium is a prominent facility located on the 5th floor of Building A at President University’s campus in Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. Named after Prof. Dr. Charles Himawan, a distinguished legal scholar and one of the university's founding figures, the auditorium serves as a central venue for various academic and extracurricular activities. This auditorium is equipped to host a range of events, including seminars, conferences, cultural performances, and student gatherings. For instance, on April 29, 2025, it was the venue for a seminar titled “Indonesia as a World Economic Power: Legal Perspective,” which featured discussions on digital innovation and economic sovereignty. Additionally, it has been used for events like table tennis tournaments, reflecting its versatility in accommodating both formal and recreational activities. The Charles Himawan Auditorium stands as a testament to President University's commitment to providing spaces that foster intellectual growth, cultural exchange, and community engagement.

President University - Facility President University Convention Center President University Convention Center is a modern venue for conferences, exhibitions, and large-scale events. The President University Convention Center (PUCC) is a modern, multi-purpose venue located within the Jababeka Education Park, Cikarang. Designed to accommodate large-scale gatherings, PUCC serves as a key facility for conferences, exhibitions, graduations, and cultural events at President University. Spanning two floors with a total area of approximately 1,900 m², PUCC can host up to 4,000 attendees in standing arrangements and around 3,600 in seated configurations. The center features a spacious ballroom with high ceilings, advanced air conditioning systems, and flexible staging options. On September 2024, PUCC was the venue for the Mandarin Career Expo, connecting students with international career opportunities, while in 2025 it hosted President University’s graduation ceremony for over 1,400 graduates. The President University Convention Center reflects the university’s dedication to supporting academic excellence, professional development, and vibrant community engagement through world-class facilities.

Student Life:
President University - Student Life In President University, students get the facilities to develop their talent by joining Club and Communities Society Club and Communities President University fosters a vibrant campus life through a wide range of society clubs and communities that encourage intellectual exchange, cultural understanding, and social awareness. These student-led groups focus on issues such as leadership, public speaking, environmental sustainability, social justice, and international affairs. By participating in these communities, students are empowered to broaden their perspectives, engage in meaningful discussions, and take part in impactful projects that benefit both the campus and the wider society. Art Club and Communities Creativity thrives at President University through its diverse art clubs and communities, which offer students a platform to explore and express their artistic talents. These groups include activities such as music, dance, theater, photography, visual arts, and creative writing. Whether performing on stage or displaying works in campus exhibitions, students are encouraged to celebrate artistic expression, collaborate across disciplines, and contribute to the university’s dynamic and inclusive cultural scene. Sport Club and Communities Promoting a healthy and active lifestyle, President University provides numerous sport clubs and communities where students can train, compete, and build teamwork. From futsal and basketball to martial arts and badminton, these clubs support both recreational and competitive levels, often representing the university in regional and national tournaments. Through sports, students develop physical fitness, discipline, resilience, and strong camaraderie. The Entrepreneurship Club at President University serves as a hub for aspiring innovators and business-minded students who are passionate about building their own ventures. Through workshops, pitch competitions, mentoring sessions, and collaboration with industry partners, the club nurtures entrepreneurial thinking and real-world problem-solving. It plays a key role in supporting students to transform ideas into viable startups and cultivating a future generation of leaders and changemakers.

Internship & Career Center:
President University - Internship & Career Center Internship & Career Center What's Next After Graduation? Our internship program serves as a facility for students to gain practical experience. By joining taking an internship program, students will learn and expose themselves to the variety of roles, functions, and abundance of opportunities in industries. This exposure generates a list of “career prospects” for the students, which will lead them to the answer of “what’s next after graduation”. The quality of our graduates are measured by the abilities to create impact for the community and are reflected from their portfolios. This portfolio is made possible because as interns, our students will be part of the innovation and problem solving process where they can showcase their discipline, personal traits, knowledge and skills to the hiring manager and grow their professional network. This portfolio will be proof of their proficiency and adds value to them as professionals. In the end, our internship goal is “employability” which may be translated as the ability of our graduates to land their dream career as how they want it, when they need it. However, employability goes beyond getting hired, it is about constantly innovating and match the competencies with what industry or hiring organizations look for in a candidate. Combined with the academic mastery, the internship portfolio often helps students secure their job before graduation and stay relevant in a dynamic job market. Internships are not just about fulfilling requirements, it is also about opening doors to the world of possibilities. Our internship journey shapes many success stories of graduates being offered with job opportunities right after they completed their internship. Ready, Set, Get Hired President University’s web-based Resume Builder guides you step by step through every section of your resume. Capture your key achievements, projects, work experience and certifications in one place. Each year the Career Center reviews your document to keep it aligned with current industry needs. The review involves personal coaching session to polish the final details, condense information, and shape a clear professional layout. The result is a concise one-page resume that you can use to apply for internships and jobs from your first semester, and one that stays competitive right up to graduation. Internship & Job Opportunity Portal One account gives you access to a curated list of internships and job openings from hundreds of partner companies. The portal ( ) is designed to boost employability by delivering a steady flow of high-quality opportunities for both students and alumni. Our annual job fair brings many companies to campus and lets you apply and interview on the same day. We also host regular Campus Hiring events, each focused on a single employer for a more targeted recruitment experience. These programs combined, create a direct path from a strong resume to a signed employment contract. International Office:
President University - International Office The International Office at President University is a dedicated department that handles all matters related to international students and mobility programs. Our primary functions include: Visa and Immigration Support Guiding visa applications, renewals, and compliance with immigration regulations, ensuring students maintain their legal status while studying. Promoting international collaboration and opportunities for students and faculty. Assisting international students with the application and admission processes related to the student mobility program, ensuring that all necessary documents and requirements are met. Detailed information is provided in our guide book for the Outbound Program and the Inbound Program. We also provide an Outbound Program Calendar for our students to find dates and deadlines for mobility programs offered by our partner universities. Universities our students have visited for Outbound Program: Countries of students that has joined our Inbound Program at President University: Offering assistance for academic, social, and personal issues, including counseling services, language assistance, and cultural exchange programs. Detailed information is provided in our guide book for International Students. Program and Events for International Students The International Office is active in conducting program and events that aim to introduce and blend cultures from and for our international students. Among them are: President University Buddy Program, where we collaborate with the Student Affairs Bureau to match international students with Indonesian students. The aim is to facilitate students in exchanging knowledge about each other's language and culture. Cultural Festival, where the students both international and local can mingle and showcase their culture in the biggest cultural show on campus. President University International Dinners, where we invite our international students to introduce their culture through cuisines that they prepare and serve for the invitees. Beyond the above programs, we also regularly host smaller events that include cultural movie night and cultural trips to various cultural and historical sites around Indonesia aimed to give international students exposure to Indonesian culture and history. Scholarships for Inbound and Outbound Mobility Program President University has provided scholarships for international students since its establishment in 2001. In the last decade, however, the International Office has provided numerous inbound international students with scholarship opportunities and programs. Scholarships such as DAAD's EMPM program, the Indonesian Aid Scholarship (TIAS), and the South East Asia Leadership program are among the ones we provide through collaborations with the relevant institutions. Aside of that, there are also many scholarship opportunities available for Indonesian students to join our outbound programs like CMK Hyundai, and numerous other partners for exchange program.

Research:
President University - Research President University actively supports innovation and intellectual property development through its research and academic community. Faculty members and students are encouraged to transform their ideas into practical solutions, many of which have been successfully registered as national and international patents. These patents reflect the university’s commitment to fostering creativity, problem-solving, and contributions to industry and society through original inventions and technologies. To enhance research excellence, President University regularly secures competitive research grants from government bodies, international institutions, and industry partners. These grants enable faculty and students to conduct impactful research across various disciplines, from science and technology to social sciences and education. The university also provides internal funding schemes to support early-stage research, prototype development, and community-based projects. Scholarly book publication is a key pillar of President University’s research output. Faculty members contribute to the academic discourse by publishing books that offer fresh perspectives, critical analyses, and in-depth explorations across diverse fields. These works are often used as references in classrooms, libraries, and academic forums, strengthening the university’s role as a knowledge-producing institution. President University promotes a strong culture of academic writing and journal publication among its faculty and students. Research articles are regularly published in reputable national and international journals, covering a wide array of topics and disciplines. This commitment to scholarly publication not only enhances the university’s research visibility but also contributes to global academic and scientific advancement.

Accounting Program at President Univ Collaborates with MNC Sekuritas and Avrist Asset Management for “Having Fund 2024” Event: The Benefits of Knowing Mutual Funds:
President University - Accounting Program at President Univ Collaborates with MNC Sekuritas and Avrist Asset Management for “Having Fund 2024” Event: The Benefits of Knowing Mutual Funds Accounting Program at President Univ Collaborates with MNC Sekuritas and Avrist Asset Management for “Having Fund 2024” Event: The Benefits of Knowing Mutual Funds On Tuesday, October 29, 2024, the Accounting Program from the Faculty of Business at President University (Presuniv) hosted the “Having Fund 2024” event in the Charles Himawan Auditorium, Building A, 5th Floor, at Presuniv’s campus in Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. This was a collaborative event between the Accounting Program, MNC Sekuritas, and Avrist Asset Management aimed to enhance students’ financial literacy under the theme The Benefits of Knowing Mutual Funds. The event focused on introducing students to the importance of investment, particularly mutual funds, as a straightforward and secure choice for beginners. Representatives from MNC Sekuritas and Avrist Asset Management provided insights on long-term investment concepts, explaining a variety of instruments suited to individual needs and risk profiles. The session featured two key speakers, Wesley Paraja Guguk, Senior Marketing APRD from MNC Sekuritas, and Tukul Rusyidi Khairi, Manager of Avrist Asset Management. Both speakers shared their knowledge and experience on mutual fund investments in a practical, and easy-to-understand manner. They also provided a step-by-step guide on starting investments with small amounts, encouraging students to manage their monthly savings wisely and incrementally.

Setsail BizAccel and International Relations Study Program, President University, Hold Sharing Session with Jonathan Davy, Co-Founder and CEO of Ecoxyztem:
President University - Setsail BizAccel and International Relations Study Program, President University, Hold Sharing Session with Jonathan Davy, Co-Founder and CEO of Ecoxyztem Setsail BizAccel and International Relations Study Program, President University, Hold Sharing Session with Jonathan Davy, Co-Founder and CEO of Ecoxyztem On Thursday, 17 October 2024, the International Relations Study Program of the Faculty of Social Sciences and Education, President University (Presuniv), in collaboration with Setsail BizAccel, a business incubator, organized a Sharing Sessions of International Relations Alumni featuring Jonathan Davy, Co-Founder and CEO of Ecoxyztem. The event was held at the Fabrication Laboratory (Fablab), President University Convention Center (PUCC), Jl. H. Usmar Ismail, Kota Jababeka, Cikarang, and focused on Jonathan’s experience in starting a business with his partners. Jonathan is an alumnus of the International Relations Program, class of 2011. Dr. Mohammad Syafi’i Anwar, Dean of the Faculty of Social Sciences and Education expressed President University’s strong support for students who aspire to develop entrepreneurial skills and have the courage to start their own businesses. “One of the career paths offered by Presuniv is entrepreneurship, as Jonathan has pursued,” he emphasized. Meanwhile, Dr. Jeanne Francoise, Head of the International Relations Program, hoped that Jonathan’s presence would have a positive impact on all students. “I hope Jonathan’s presence will inspire students,” she remarked. In his presentation, Jonathan shared that he originally wanted to become a diplomat. However, his dream was halted as there was a two-year moratorium on diplomat recruitment when he graduated from Presuniv. This situation forced him to change direction. Together with his friends, he decided to start his own business, leading to the establishment of Ecoxyztem. Jonathan emphasized the importance of self-exploration during college. “Try to find out what you are passionate about. Once you find it, keep exploring. From there, you might discover and eventually create your own business,” Jonathan advised. Ruhmaya Wathoni, B.BA, M.Sc., Director of Setsail BizAccel Presuniv and moderator of the event, agreed with Jonathan’s view. She added, “Starting your own business is not easy, but at Presuniv, we have various facilities to help students build their businesses. Setsail BizAccel is ready to assist students in starting their ventures.” (Graciella V, PR Team. Photo: Graciella V.)

Epicor Indonesia Initiates ERP Application Training for President University Students:
President University - Epicor Indonesia Initiates ERP Application Training for President University Students Epicor Indonesia Initiates ERP Application Training for President University Students Currently, technology plays a very strategic role in the business world. Almost all companies have implemented technology, both hardware and software, in their business processes. To anticipate this development, President University (Presuniv) collaborates with technology companies to prepare its students so that after graduation they have skills that match industry needs. One such collaboration is with PT Epicor Software Indonesia. Through this collaboration, Epicor provided assistance worth US$170,000 in the form of Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software licenses and training programs for Presuniv students. According to Genta Sahuri M.Sc., an IS Study Program lecturer and ERP Project course instructor, “The ERP course and training are indeed aimed at making Presuniv stay one step ahead of other universities. Moreover, Presuniv is located in the Jababeka industrial area with thousands of companies within it. So, we can directly see real-world cases and the application of technology at these companies.” ERP is the most widely used application by companies worldwide. Through this application, companies can manage and integrate all their business processes. One key factor in implementing ERP is that all data and business processes within the company must be integrated into one platform and transformed into information. This information must be accessible by various departments or business units within the company. With complete and real-time information, companies can quickly make important business decisions. The training in the use of ERP applications is provided to Presuniv students. According to Andi Mulyawan, ERP applications are widely used by companies in the manufacturing industry. “And, in the Jababeka industrial area, there are around 2,000 companies, most of which are in the manufacturing sector. So, if Presuniv graduates are proficient in using ERP applications, they will be quickly absorbed by these companies,” explained Andi. To all participants, Andi advised them to truly utilize this training to develop their skills and knowledge in using the ERP applications. “Through this training, Presuniv has opened the door as wide as possible for all students to have skills and knowledge that meet industry needs. So, take advantage of this opportunity by learning and practicing earnestly,” said Andi. (Faisal Rabbani, PR team. Photo: Faisal Rabbani)

President Univ Collaborates with Jababeka and HRI Group to Host 2024 Mandarin Career Expo:
President University - President Univ Collaborates with Jababeka and HRI Group to Host 2024 Mandarin Career Expo President Univ Collaborates with Jababeka and HRI Group to Host 2024 Mandarin Career Expo On Friday, September 13, 2024, President University (Presuniv), in collaboration with PT Jababeka Tbk, Chindo Business Center, and HRI Group, held the 2024 Mandarin Career Expo at the President University Convention Center (PUCC), Jl. H. Usmar Ismail, Kota Jababeka, Cikarang. The event aimed to facilitate student’s and graduate’s careers and to raise awareness about the importance of learning multiple languages for their future career growth. In his opening speech, Handa S. Abidin, S.H., LL.M., Ph.D., Rector of Presuniv, discussed the increasingly competitive job market. “Today, many students and graduates are fluent in both Indonesian and English. To enhance competitiveness, we need to learn other languages, such as Mandarin,” Handa explained. He added, “The Internship and Career Center at Presuniv has received many requests for students proficient in Mandarin, which reflects the demand for Mandarin-speaking workers in Indonesia today.” The 2024 Mandarin Career Expo also featured performances by Presuniv students. Huang Yiting, a student from the 2023 Accounting study program and a native of China, showcased her talent in traditional Chinese dance in front of the attendees. Additionally, Jocelyn Shandy from the 2023 Communication study program displayed her Mandarin skills by performing the song Yue Liang Dai Biao Wo De Xin by Teresa Teng. (Graciella V, PR Team. Photo: Graciella V)

Study Program of Accounting, President Univ, Holds Seminar on the Role of Accountants in an AI-Driven Business with CPA Australia and Glico Indonesia:
President University - Study Program of Accounting, President Univ, Holds Seminar on the Role of Accountants in an AI-Driven Business with CPA Australia and Glico Indonesia Study Program of Accounting, President Univ, Holds Seminar on the Role of Accountants in an AI-Driven Business with CPA Australia and Glico Indonesia According to the Head of the Study Program of Accounting, Mila Austria Reyes, CPA, MBA, it is crucial for students to keep a close watch on the development of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology. “What you learn during this seminar is vital for career development, including sharpening the vision of the career you will pursue in the future, especially after graduation,” said Mila. The event featured two speakers: Oscar Syarif, Chief Operating Officer of Ledgerowl, and Melisa Yosefine, Financial Controller and Commercial Business Partner of PT Glico Indonesia. Melisa emphasized that students should not fear AI taking over their jobs. “Companies, whether big or small, will always need accountants because accounting is the language of business,” she said. Meanwhile, Oscar explained that AI is merely a tool to assist in work. “We cannot continue to work using traditional methods. We must evolve and adapt to technological advancements. This is crucial for our careers,” he stressed. (Graciella V, PR Team. Report: Evander Stevanus. Photos: Damar Satriawan).

Angel Kusuma Ajinata, President University Student, Shared Hands-On ERP Learning Experience at Manufacturing Innovation Day 2026 in Front of Industry Leaders:
President University - Angel Kusuma Ajinata, President University Student, Shared Hands-On ERP Learning Experience at Manufacturing Innovation Day 2026 in Front of Industry Leaders Angel Kusuma Ajinata, President University Student, Shared Hands-On ERP Learning Experience at Manufacturing Innovation Day 2026 in Front of Industry Leaders Wednesday, 2 April 2026 — President University, in collaboration with EPICOR, held the Epicor & President University Manufacturing Innovation Day in Alliance with Indonesia 4.0 at the Fabrication Lab (FabLab), President University Convention Center, President University Main Campus, Jababeka City, Cikarang, Bekasi. During the event, Angel Kusuma Ajinata, a President University student, shared her personal learning experience in the Information System study program, particularly in the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) concentration. She explained that she began her internship in her second year, allowing her to gain practical experience earlier and eventually contribute to EPICOR-related projects, including involvement in client-oriented programs. She noted that these experiences have been valuable in bridging academic learning with real-world application. She also emphasized the importance of hands-on learning, stating, “ERP is one of the main concentrations in the Information System study program. If we only learn the theory, it would be much more difficult to fully understand. I am personally grateful to study at President University, where we are provided with systems that allow us to implement what we have learned.”

IMPACT Core Values and Slogans:
President University Core Values: IMPACT. I stands for International: A global academic environment with international students and alumni from 50 countries. M stands for Medium of Communication in English: All learning and communication are conducted in English, ensuring global readiness. P stands for Portfolio-Building Curriculum: An extensive curriculum designed to help students build a strong portfolio for future careers or graduate studies. A stands for Absolutely Worth It, Value for Money: The extensive student benefits and diverse scholarship opportunities make choosing President University a worthwhile decision. C stands for Culture: Creating Moments of Happiness: A vibrant campus culture that nurtures happiness, creativity, and lifelong friendships. T stands for Targeted Career Preparation: A focused career development approach that prepares students with practical skills and industry-relevant experience aligned to their future professions. Slogans and Nicknames: Welcome to the most international university in Indonesia. President University is also famously known as the most roblox university in indonesia.
